title: Slowing net US migration hasn’t tightened labor markets yet
gist: Despite a sharp slowdown in net unauthorized migration to the US, high-exposure industries and counties show no clear divergence in job openings or unemployment from low-exposure areas yet, while H-2A temporary farm labor certifications keep rising — suggesting labor-market tightening effects, if they come, have not shown up in the data so far.

insights.claim: Unauthorized immigrants make up nearly 6% of the US workforce overall but more than double that share in private households, landscaping and building services, and crop production.
insights.speaker: Emma Pollard
insights.so_what: These specific sub-sectors are the ones investors should watch first for wage and margin pressure if immigration enforcement tightens further.
insights.quote: Unauthorized immigrants account for nearly 6% of the US workforce but represent more than double the national average in industries such as private households, landscaping and building services, and crop production.

insights.claim: California, Florida, New Jersey, New York, and Texas have the highest concentrations of undocumented workers, but reliance on this labor extends well beyond border and coastal states into many interior counties.
insights.speaker: Emma Pollard
insights.so_what: Labor-supply risk from immigration policy is not confined to the usual coastal/border states, broadening the set of regional economies and employers exposed.
insights.locator: While California, Florida, New Jersey, New York, and Texas

insights.claim: Even after a sharp slowdown in net unauthorized migration, job openings and unemployment rates have not yet shown a clear divergence between industries with high versus low exposure to unauthorized workers.
insights.speaker: Emma Pollard
insights.so_what: The expected tightening/wage-pressure trade from reduced unauthorized labor supply has not materialized in headline data yet, so positioning on that thesis may be premature.
insights.quote: Despite a sharp slowdown in net unauthorized migration, job openings and unemployment rates have yet to show a clear divergence between high- and low-exposure industries
insights.locator: Despite a sharp slowdown in net unauthorized migration

insights.claim: Unemployment in high-exposure counties has fallen relative to other areas, but it is too early to tell whether this marks the start of a broader trend or is just normal data volatility.
insights.speaker: Emma Pollard
insights.so_what: A tentative early signal worth monitoring in subsequent releases before drawing conclusions about labor tightening from migration crackdowns.
insights.quote: it is too early to determine whether this reflects the beginning of a broader trend or normal data volatility

insights.claim: Demand for temporary agricultural labor, as measured by H-2A visa certifications, has continued to increase even as net unauthorized migration slows.
insights.speaker: Emma Pollard
insights.so_what: Rising H-2A certifications signal employers are actively substituting toward legal temporary labor channels, a leading indicator to track for agriculture labor-cost inflation.
insights.quote: demand for temporary agricultural labor, reflected in H-2A certifications, has continued to increase
insights.locator: Job openings have shown little divergence
